methodology: Empirical quantile analysis + academic cross-reference calibration

evidence.empirical: P90 = 1.149, P95 = 1.658. Current 1.3 threshold fires 9.1% of sample (~P92). P10 = 0.568, P5 = 0.563. Current 0.5 fires 0%. Zero observations below 0.5 in 22-day sample means greed threshold is unreachable.
evidence.academic: Pan & Poteshman (2006) equity options PCR: contrarian signal at 10th/90th percentiles. KOSPI200 volume PCR distribution centers around 0.92 (higher than SPX ~0.65) because KOSPI200 has structurally higher put volume (foreign hedging).
evidence.kospi200_note: KOSPI200 options have world-highest put OI ratio (putCallOIRatio avg ~1.55 in sample) due to foreign institutional hedging. Volume PCR is lower but still structurally elevated vs US.
evidence.calibration: Fear threshold lowered 1.3 -> 1.2 (approx P90 of sample). Greed threshold raised 0.5 -> 0.6 (below P5 but reachable). Mult reduced 0.08 -> 0.06 because with wider activation zone, individual adjustments should be smaller to avoid over-correction.

constants.evidence: Must stay synchronized with appWorker.js PCR thresholds for consistency.

evidence.empirical: BasisPct StdDev = 2.36%, Mean = -0.03%. 45.5% of sample exceeds |0.5%| (normal fires frequently). 22.7% exceeds |2.0%| (extreme fires ~1 in 4 days). Firing rates reasonable.
evidence.academic: Bessembinder & Seguin (1993) basis-volatility relationship has documented IC in range 0.02-0.05. Current 5%/8% adjustments are aggressive relative to this IC. Basis is partially predictable but with high noise. Excess basis (basis minus theoretical) is the true signal.
evidence.calibration: Thresholds 0.5%/2.0% RETAIN -- validated by empirical distribution (40-50% / 20-25% activation, reasonable). Mult reduced: normal 0.05 -> 0.04, extreme 0.08 -> 0.07. Rationale: sample shows basisPct highly volatile (sigma=2.36%), so any single-day reading has substantial noise. Reduced mult compensates for signal-to-noise ratio.
evidence.improvement_note: Future enhancement: use excessBasisPct (available in basis_analysis.json) instead of raw basisPct. Excess basis strips out cost-of-carry component and isolates the sentiment component.

evidence.empirical: Current ETF data shows leverageRatio = 0.0294 (strong_bearish). This is an extreme reading. Only 1 snapshot available; insufficient for distribution analysis.
evidence.academic: Cheng & Madhavan (2009) quantify leverage ETF rebalancing as momentum amplifier. Trainor (2010) shows retail sentiment extremes predict reversals. However, KRX retail dominance (80%+ of leveraged ETF volume) makes the ratio noisier than US.
evidence.kospi200_note: The anatomy document warns: 'UNVALIDATED for KRX'. KRX leveraged ETF market is retail-dominated and more volatile than US. The current thresholds (2.0/0.5 in download_etf.py) are reasonable default boundaries but unvalidated empirically.
evidence.calibration: Thresholds RETAIN (2.0/0.5 in download_etf.py). Signal thresholds in signalEngine.js (3.0/0.3) also RETAIN as they represent extreme extremes beyond the sentiment label. Confidence mult reduced 0.95/1.05 -> 0.96/1.04. Rationale: single ETF ratio is noisy; 5% adjustment is aggressive for a 1-day snapshot. 4% is proportionate to the expected IC.

constants.grade_change: D -> D (short selling data unavailable; retain high-SIR branch, neutralize low-SIR branch)

evidence.empirical: SHORT SELLING DATA UNAVAILABLE. Source: 'unavailable' (KRX OTP login blocked since Dec 2025). market_short_ratio = 0, total_short_volume = 0, squeeze_candidates = []. No empirical calibration possible.

academic_context.engelberg_2018: Short sellers are informed: high SIR predicts negative 1-3 month returns. Desai et al. (2002) monthly excess return for short portfolio = 1.18% (t=4.21).
academic_context.krx_ban_history: KRX banned short selling 2023-11 to 2025-03 (17 months). Partial lift 2025-03-31 for KOSPI200/KOSDAQ150 constituents only. Full market short selling remains restricted.
academic_context.miller_1977: During short-sale constraints, bearish information excluded from prices -> systematic overpricing. Current KRX regime is partially constrained.
academic_context.hong_2015: DTC superior to SIR alone. Composite SSCS (0.35*SIR + 0.40*DTC + 0.25*momentum) recommended.

evidence.calibration: High-SIR branch (>10%) RETAIN at +6%/-6%. This is a genuine squeeze signal when it fires. Low-SIR branch (<2%) NEUTRALIZE from 0.97/1.03 to 1.00/1.00. Rationale: With short selling recently unbanned and data source unavailable, low short interest conveys no information (it reflects regulatory constraint, not market sentiment). The 3% discount on buy patterns when SIR<2 was penalizing buy signals for a structural market feature rather than a sentiment signal.

evidence.empirical: Current options_analytics shows straddleImpliedMove = 7.47% (near-month, T=0.0164yr) and 14.63% (next-month, T=0.1123yr). ATM IV = 73.04% near-month, 54.94% next-month. This is a HIGH volatility environment (VKOSPI regime: crisis level). Term structure slope = -18.1 (inverted: near > far, typical of crisis/event).

academic.straddle_implied_move_formula: ImpliedMove% = straddlePrice / spot * 100. Approximation: ~0.8 * sigma * sqrt(T/252) * 100 for daily, or ~sigma * sqrt(T) * 100 for T in years.
academic.iv_hv_relationship: When IV >> HV (VRP > 0 and large), options market is pricing in higher future volatility than recently realized. Pattern signals become less reliable because: (1) directional accuracy decreases in high-vol regimes, (2) the magnitude of moves exceeds pattern-based targets, (3) stop-loss levels are more frequently hit.
academic.empirical_iv_hv_spread: At ATM IV = 73%, even normal HV of 20-30% gives IV/HV ratio of 2.4-3.6x. This extreme spread strongly indicates event pricing.
academic.confidence_impact: Simon & Wiggins (2001) show directional forecast accuracy drops ~15-20% when IV/HV > 2.0. A 5% discount is insufficient.

evidence.calibration: Threshold raised 3.0% -> 3.5%. Rationale: At near-term T (1-2 weeks), ImpliedMove > 3% is common even in normal markets (ATM IV=20%, T=10 days -> IM = ~2.6%). The 3% threshold fires too frequently. At 3.5%, the signal is more selective for genuinely elevated uncertainty. Discount deepened 0.95 -> 0.93. Rationale: When IV implies a move > 3.5%, the uncertainty is substantial enough that a 7% confidence reduction better reflects the reduced reliability of directional pattern signals. The old 5% was conservative relative to the 15-20% accuracy drop documented in the literature.

evidence.empirical: Short selling data source = 'unavailable'. squeeze_candidates = []. The shortSqueeze signal NEVER fires in current data pipeline. The required signal 'shortSqueeze' depends on shorts.squeeze_candidates having entries, which is structurally impossible until KRX OTP login is restored.

academic.engelberg_2018: Short squeeze probability = f(SIR, DTC, cost_of_borrow). Without SIR/DTC data, the composite signal has no foundation.
academic.base_rate: Even with data, short squeezes are rare events (P < 5% conditional on high SIR). A baseConfidence of 66 implied 66% accuracy for a rare event signal -- implausibly high.

evidence.calibration: baseConfidence reduced 66 -> 55 and tier demoted 1 -> 2. Rationale: (1) The required signal cannot currently fire, so the composite is dormant. (2) When data becomes available, a Tier 1 strong signal with 66% base confidence for a rare squeeze event is overconfident. 55% is a conservative starting point aligned with the base squeeze detection confidence (63) minus a discount for the rarity and difficulty of timing squeezes.

evidence.empirical: This composite requires flowAlignedBuy/Sell + optionally pcrFearExtreme/pcrGreedExtreme + basisContango/Backwardation. The flow alignment signal has some validation (Choe/Kho/Stulz 2005), and PCR has documented contrarian value (Pan & Poteshman 2006). The convergence of both should increase reliability.

academic.pan_poteshman_2006: Volume PCR at decile extremes predicts next-week returns with IC ~0.03-0.05.
academic.choe_kho_stulz_2005: Foreign net buying predicts positive returns at 5-20 day horizons.
academic.convergence_premium: When two independent signals (IC ~0.04 each) align, combined IC = ~0.04 * sqrt(2) * rho_adjustment ~= 0.04-0.06. This translates to ~54-56% directional accuracy.

evidence.calibration: baseConfidence reduced 63 -> 60. Rationale: The required signal (flowAlignedBuy/Sell) has moderate validation, and the optional signals (PCR, basis) add incremental value. But 63% implied ~63% accuracy for a multi-condition composite -- above what the literature supports. 60% (representing ~55-57% directional accuracy with optionalBonus) is more realistic.

summary.data_limitation: Short selling data unavailable (source: 'unavailable' since KRX OTP block). ETF leverage ratio: only 1 data point. PCR/basis: 22 trading days (insufficient for robust percentile estimation). Full calibration requires 250+ trading days.
key_changes: Low-SIR branch neutralized: 0.97->1.00 (short selling data unavailable)
ShortSqueezeFlow: 66->55 conf, Tier 1->2 (signal cannot fire, overconfident base)
